title: Introduction to Weevio Cloud
description: Learn about Weevio Cloud, the comprehensive multi-tenant SaaS platform for PIMS management and integrations.
content:
Weevio Cloud is a comprehensive multi-tenant SaaS platform designed to streamline PIMS management, customer engagement, and e-commerce operations for repair shops and service businesses.

## What's Inside

Weevio Cloud consists of five integrated applications:

* **Store Management** - Manage orders, service repair orders (SROs), customers, and inventory
* **Customer Portal** - Self-service portal for customers to track orders, pay invoices, and manage SROs
* **Appointment Scheduler** - Embed appointment booking on your website with GSX integration
* **GSX Integration** - Automated email workflows and appointment synchronization with Apple GSX
* **Shopify Integration** - Sync inventory, orders, and enhance your store with variant selectors and cross-sell widgets

## Get Started

title: Quick Start Guide
description: Get up and running with Weevio Cloud in minutes.
content:
This guide will help you get started with Weevio Cloud quickly and efficiently.

## Prerequisites

Before you begin, ensure you have:

* Access to your Weevio Cloud account
* PIMS Platinum/Gold credentials (if using PIMS integration)
* Administrative access to your systems

## Step 1: Account Setup

1. Log in to your Weevio Cloud account
2. Complete your organization profile
3. Configure your basic settings

## Step 2: Choose Your Apps

Select which Weevio Cloud applications you want to use:

* **Store Management** - Start managing orders and customers
* **Customer Portal** - Enable self-service for customers
* **Appointment Scheduler** - Set up appointment booking
* **GSX Integration** - Connect with Apple GSX
* **Shopify Integration** - Link your Shopify store

## Step 3: Configure Integrations

Set up integrations based on your selected apps:

* Configure PIMS connection
* Set up GSX credentials (if applicable)
* Connect your Shopify store (if applicable)

## Step 4: Customize Settings

Adjust settings to match your business needs:

* Branding and appearance
* Notification preferences
* User permissions
* Workflow automation

## Next Steps

Explore detailed documentation for each application:

* [Store Management](/docs/store)
* [Customer Portal](/docs/customer-portal)
* [Appointment Scheduler](/docs/appointment-scheduler)
* [GSX Integration](/docs/gsx)
* [Shopify Integration](/docs/shopify)

## Need Help?

Visit our [Support](/docs/support) section or contact our team for assistance.

title: System Requirements
description: Technical requirements for using Weevio Cloud platform.
content:
Learn about the technical requirements and compatibility for Weevio Cloud.

## Browser Support

Weevio Cloud supports the latest versions of:

* Google Chrome (recommended)
* Mozilla Firefox
* Safari
* Microsoft Edge

## Network Requirements

* Stable internet connection
* HTTPS support
* Ports 443 and 80 accessible

## Integration Requirements

### PIMS Integration

* PIMS Platinum or PIMS Gold
* Valid PIMS credentials
* Network access to PIMS server

### GSX Integration

* Apple Authorized Service Provider status
* Valid GSX credentials
* GSX API access

### Shopify Integration

* Active Shopify store
* Shopify admin access
* API access permissions

## Customer Portal Requirements

For customers accessing the portal:

* Modern web browser
* Email address for notifications
* Mobile-responsive design support

## Appointment Scheduler

For embedding on your website:

* Website with ability to embed iframes or scripts
* HTTPS-enabled website (recommended)

## Security Requirements

* TLS 1.2 or higher
* Modern SSL certificates
* Secure authentication methods

## Performance Recommendations

For optimal performance:

* Broadband internet connection (5 Mbps or higher)
* Modern devices (released within last 5 years)
* JavaScript enabled

## Need Help?

Contact our [Support](/docs/support/contact) team if you have questions about compatibility.

title: What is Weevio Cloud?
description: Overview of Weevio Cloud platform capabilities and features.
content:
Weevio Cloud is a comprehensive multi-tenant SaaS platform built to help repair shops and service businesses manage their operations more efficiently.

## Key Features

### Multi-Application Platform

Weevio Cloud integrates five powerful applications:

1. **Store Management** - Complete control over orders, SROs, customers, and inventory
2. **Customer Portal** - Self-service capabilities for your customers
3. **Appointment Scheduler** - Seamless appointment booking with GSX integration
4. **GSX Integration** - Automated workflows for Apple authorized service providers
5. **Shopify Integration** - E-commerce synchronization and enhancement tools

## Platform Benefits

* **Centralized Management** - All your business operations in one place
* **Customer Self-Service** - Reduce support workload with portal access
* **Seamless Integrations** - Connect with PIMS, GSX, and Shopify
* **Automated Workflows** - Save time with email automation and sync features
* **Multi-Tenant Architecture** - Scalable and secure for businesses of all sizes

## Who is it For?

Weevio Cloud is ideal for:

* Apple Authorized Service Providers
* Independent repair shops
* Businesses using PIMS Platinum/Gold
* Retailers with Shopify stores
* Service businesses needing customer portals

## Next Steps

Ready to get started? Check out the [Quick Start Guide](/docs/introduction/quick-start).

title: PunchOut Admin Setup
description: Configure customer tagging in Shopify to enable PunchOut fromIdentity pricing resolution.
content:
This guide explains how to configure Shopify customer accounts to enable PunchOut fromIdentity pricing. This is required for buyers to see their custom pricing when browsing through SAP Ariba or other procurement systems.

## Overview

For PunchOut custom pricing to work, you must **tag Shopify customers** with their SAP Ariba buyer ID. The system uses these tags to map the `fromIdentity` parameter (sent by SAP Ariba) to the correct Shopify customer account.

**Key Concept**: When a buyer clicks through from SAP Ariba with `?fromIdentity=AN01631249352`, the system searches for a Shopify customer tagged with `Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352` and displays that customer's pricing.

## Prerequisites

Before you begin:

* \[ ] Access to Shopify Admin dashboard
* \[ ] Customer account management permissions
* \[ ] List of SAP Ariba buyer IDs from your procurement team
* \[ ] Understanding of your customer pricing structure

## Customer Tagging Requirements

### Required Tag: Buyer ID

Every PunchOut customer **must** have a tag that identifies their SAP Ariba buyer ID.

**Supported Tag Formats**:

The system recognizes any of these tag prefixes (case-insensitive):

```
Ariba Buyer: [BUYER_ID]
SAP Ariba Buyer: [BUYER_ID]
PunchOut Buyer: [BUYER_ID]
```

**Examples of valid tags**:

```
Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
SAP Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
PunchOut Buyer: AN01631249352
ariba_buyer:AN01631249352
ARIBA BUYER: AN01631249352
ariba buyer:AN01631249352
```

**Tag flexibility**:

* **Case-insensitive**: "Ariba Buyer" = "ariba buyer" = "ARIBA BUYER"
* **Space/underscore flexible**: "Ariba Buyer:" = "ariba\_buyer:" = "ariba buyer:"
* **Spaces around colon**: "Ariba Buyer: ID" = "Ariba Buyer:ID"

### Optional Tag: Price Code

If you use tiered pricing or price lists, you can add a price code tag:

**Format**:

```
PRICE CODE: [PRICE_CODE_NAME]
Price Code: [PRICE_CODE_NAME]
price_code:[PRICE_CODE_NAME]
```

**Examples**:

```
PRICE CODE: IPG_TIER_1
Price Code: CORPORATE_DISCOUNT
price_code:VIP_PRICING
```

**Use cases**:

* Multiple customers sharing the same price tier
* Different pricing levels (Tier 1, Tier 2, VIP, etc.)
* Customer group pricing
* Regional pricing variations

## Step-by-Step Setup

### Step 1: Gather Required Information

Before tagging customers, collect:

1. **Buyer IDs from SAP Ariba**
   * Contact your procurement team or SAP Ariba administrator
   * Buyer IDs typically look like: `AN01631249352`
   * You need one buyer ID per B2B customer

2. **Customer mapping**
   * Match each SAP Ariba buyer ID to a Shopify customer account
   * Create a spreadsheet: Customer Name | Shopify Email | SAP Ariba Buyer ID

3. **Price codes** (if applicable)
   * Determine which price tier each customer belongs to
   * Use consistent naming (e.g., "TIER\_1", "TIER\_2", "VIP")

### Step 2: Access Shopify Admin

### Step 3: Add Tags to Customer

### Step 4: Verify Tags

After saving, verify the tags appear correctly:

1. **Check tag list**: Tags should appear in the customer's profile
2. **Check formatting**: Verify the buyer ID is exact (no typos)
3. **Check uniqueness**: Ensure no other customer has the same buyer ID tag

## Tag Management Best Practices

### Buyer ID Uniqueness

**Rule**: Each buyer ID should be unique to one Shopify customer.

**What happens if duplicate**:

* The system may return the first matching customer
* Pricing may be inconsistent
* Error logged in system

**Best practice**:

* Maintain a master spreadsheet of buyer ID → customer mappings
* Audit tags periodically to check for duplicates
* Remove old tags when buyer IDs change

### Price Code Sharing

**Rule**: Multiple customers can share the same price code.

**Example scenario**:

```
Customer A: Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352, PRICE CODE: TIER_1
Customer B: Ariba Buyer: AN01631249353, PRICE CODE: TIER_1
Customer C: Ariba Buyer: AN01631249354, PRICE CODE: TIER_2
```

Customers A and B see the same tier pricing, Customer C sees different pricing.

### Tag Naming Conventions

For consistency across your customer base:

**Buyer ID tags** - Use consistent prefix:

* ✅ Always use "Ariba Buyer:" (recommended)
* ⚠️ Don't mix prefixes randomly (confusing for audits)

**Price code tags** - Use consistent format:

* ✅ "PRICE CODE: TIER\_1", "PRICE CODE: TIER\_2"
* ✅ Use underscores instead of spaces in code names
* ❌ Avoid special characters like @, #, $

**Example good naming**:

```
Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
PRICE CODE: CORPORATE_A
PRICE CODE: REGION_WEST
```

**Example poor naming**:

```
ariba AN01631249352      (missing prefix)
Price: Corporate-A%      (inconsistent format, special char)
```

## Bulk Tagging

### Using Shopify Bulk Editor

For tagging many customers at once:

<Note type="warning">
  **CSV Formatting**: Be careful with commas in tags. If your buyer ID or price code contains a comma, you'll need to escape it properly or use quotes in the CSV.
</Note>

### Using Shopify API (Advanced)

For developers or automation:

```javascript
// Example: Add tags via Shopify Admin API
const customerId = 'gid://shopify/Customer/7234567890';
const tags = [
  'Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352',
  'PRICE CODE: IPG_TIER_1'
];

// GraphQL mutation
mutation {
  customerUpdate(input: {
    id: "${customerId}",
    tags: ${JSON.stringify(tags)}
  }) {
    customer {
      id
      tags
    }
    userErrors {
      field
      message
    }
  }
}
```

See Shopify's [Customer API documentation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/customerUpdate) for details.

## Testing the Configuration

After tagging customers, test that the integration works:

### Method 1: Use PunchOut Simulator

1. **Access simulator**: [PunchOut Cloud Simulator](https://pcsf.cloud.punchoutexpress.com/simulator/cart/cxml.php)
2. **Enter your store URL** with test buyer ID:
   ```
   https://yourstore.com?fromIdentity=AN01631249352
   ```
3. **Open in browser** and verify custom pricing appears

### Method 2: Manual URL Testing

### Method 3: Check sessionStorage

In browser developer tools:

```javascript
// Check if fromIdentity was captured
sessionStorage.getItem('weevio_fromIdentity');

// Expected output:
// {"value":"AN01631249352","timestamp":1699118400000}
```

## Common Configuration Issues

### Issue: Pricing Not Resolving

**Symptoms**: Buyer sees public pricing instead of custom pricing

**Possible causes**:

1. **Customer not tagged**: Verify tag exists in Shopify customer profile
2. **Typo in buyer ID**: Double-check buyer ID matches exactly (case-sensitive for ID part)
3. **Wrong tag format**: Ensure tag starts with approved prefix
4. **Duplicate buyer IDs**: Check if multiple customers have same buyer ID tag

**Solution**:

1. Go to Shopify Admin → Customers
2. Search for customer by buyer ID (use tag search)
3. Verify tag format: `Ariba Buyer: [EXACT_ID]`
4. Correct any typos or formatting issues
5. Save and test again

### Issue: Multiple Customers Match

**Symptoms**: Error in logs about multiple matching customers

**Cause**: Two or more Shopify customers have the same buyer ID tag

**Solution**:

1. Search Shopify customers for the duplicate buyer ID
2. Determine which customer is correct
3. Remove the buyer ID tag from incorrect customer(s)
4. Consider archiving old/inactive customer accounts

### Issue: Price Code Not Applied

**Symptoms**: Customer pricing works but price code pricing doesn't

**Possible causes**:

1. **Price code tag missing**: Customer has buyer ID tag but no price code tag
2. **Price code not configured**: Price code exists in tag but not in pricing system
3. **Typo in price code**: Price code tag doesn't match pricing system

**Solution**:

1. Verify customer has both tags: buyer ID AND price code
2. Check price code spelling matches pricing system exactly
3. Confirm price code is active in your pricing configuration

### Issue: Tags Not Saving

**Symptoms**: Tags disappear after saving customer profile

**Possible causes**:

1. **Shopify permissions issue**: User account lacks customer edit permissions
2. **Tag validation error**: Tag contains invalid characters
3. **Browser issue**: Cache or session problem

**Solution**:

1. Verify you have "Customers" permission in Shopify Admin
2. Remove any special characters from tags
3. Try a different browser or clear browser cache
4. Contact Shopify support if problem persists

## Updating and Managing Tags

### Changing Buyer IDs

If a customer's SAP Ariba buyer ID changes:

<Note type="info">
  **Backend cache**: It may take up to 15 minutes for the change to take effect due to backend caching. Test after 15 minutes if pricing doesn't update immediately.
</Note>

### Removing PunchOut Access

To disable PunchOut pricing for a customer:

1. Open customer profile in Shopify Admin
2. Remove the buyer ID tag (e.g., `Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352`)
3. Optionally remove price code tag
4. Save changes

The customer will no longer see custom pricing via PunchOut (but can still log in normally if they have a Shopify account).

### Archiving Old Customers

When a B2B customer relationship ends:

**Option 1: Remove Tags Only**

* Remove buyer ID and price code tags
* Keep customer account active (for historical orders)

**Option 2: Archive Customer**

* Remove tags first
* Disable or archive the customer account in Shopify
* Maintains order history but prevents new purchases

## Tag Audit Checklist

Perform regular tag audits (monthly or quarterly):

* \[ ] **Check for duplicates**: Search for duplicate buyer ID tags
* \[ ] **Verify active customers**: Ensure all active B2B customers are tagged
* \[ ] **Remove inactive tags**: Clean up tags from old/inactive customers
* \[ ] **Standardize formats**: Ensure consistent tag formatting across all customers
* \[ ] **Test sample customers**: Verify pricing resolution works for 5-10 random customers
* \[ ] **Update documentation**: Maintain spreadsheet of buyer ID → customer mappings
* \[ ] **Review price codes**: Verify price codes match current pricing structure

## Integration with Other Systems

### Syncing with ERP/CRM

If you use an ERP or CRM system:

**Best practice**:

* Maintain buyer ID mappings in your ERP/CRM
* Use Shopify API to automatically sync tags
* Set up periodic sync (daily or weekly)
* Log sync results for auditing

**Example workflow**:

1. ERP contains: Customer → SAP Ariba Buyer ID → Price Tier
2. Nightly script queries ERP for B2B customers
3. Script updates Shopify customer tags via API
4. Script logs any errors or mismatches

### SAP Ariba Integration

**Coordinate with procurement team**:

* Request list of active buyer IDs from SAP Ariba admin
* Confirm buyer ID format (usually starts with "AN")
* Set up process for new buyer ID notifications
* Establish escalation path for mismatches

## Security Considerations

### Access Control

**Who should have tagging permissions**:

* ✅ Shopify admin with customer management role
* ✅ B2B account manager
* ✅ Customer service team lead

**Who should NOT have tagging permissions**:

* ❌ General customer service reps (risk of errors)
* ❌ External contractors (unless supervised)

### Tag Confidentiality

**Buyer IDs are semi-sensitive**:

* Not personally identifiable information
* But could be used to impersonate buyer in PunchOut
* Don't publish buyer IDs on public websites
* Limit access to spreadsheets containing mappings

### Audit Logging

**Recommended practices**:

* Enable Shopify audit logs (if available in your plan)
* Track who adds/removes buyer ID tags
* Log tag changes in external system
* Review changes quarterly

## Advanced Configurations

### Multiple Buyer IDs per Customer

**Scenario**: One Shopify customer has multiple SAP Ariba buyer IDs.

**Solution**: Add multiple buyer ID tags:

```
Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
Ariba Buyer: AN01631249353
PRICE CODE: TIER_1
```

Both buyer IDs will resolve to the same Shopify customer.

### Temporary Price Codes

**Scenario**: Customer gets temporary promotional pricing.

**Solution**:

1. Add temporary price code tag: `PRICE CODE: PROMO_Q4_2024`
2. Configure pricing system for the promo code
3. After promo ends, change tag to: `PRICE CODE: STANDARD_TIER`

### Regional Pricing

**Scenario**: Same customer sees different pricing based on ship-to region.

**Solution**: Use compound price codes:

```
Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
PRICE CODE: TIER_1_US_WEST
```

Configure your pricing system to handle region-specific codes.

## Troubleshooting Resources

## Support

If you encounter issues not covered in this guide:

**Contact Weevio Support** with:

* Customer's Shopify ID or email
* Customer's SAP Ariba buyer ID
* Screenshot of customer tags in Shopify
* Description of expected vs. actual behavior
* Any error messages from browser console

**Include test details**:

* Test URL you used
* Browser and device
* Whether tracker script is installed (check with developer)

* **Case-insensitive**: "Ariba Buyer" = "ariba buyer" = "ARIBA BUYER"
* **Space/underscore flexible**: "Ariba Buyer:" = "ariba\_buyer:" = "ariba buyer:"
* **Spaces around colon**: "Ariba Buyer: ID" = "Ariba Buyer:ID"

### Optional Tag: Price Code

If you use tiered pricing or price lists, you can add a price code tag:

**Format**:

PRICE CODE: IPG_TIER_1
Price Code: CORPORATE_DISCOUNT
price_code:VIP_PRICING

**Use cases**:

* Multiple customers sharing the same price tier
* Different pricing levels (Tier 1, Tier 2, VIP, etc.)
* Customer group pricing
* Regional pricing variations

## Step-by-Step Setup

### Step 1: Gather Required Information

Before tagging customers, collect:

1. **Buyer IDs from SAP Ariba**
   * Contact your procurement team or SAP Ariba administrator
   * Buyer IDs typically look like:

* You need one buyer ID per B2B customer

2. **Customer mapping**
   * Match each SAP Ariba buyer ID to a Shopify customer account
   * Create a spreadsheet: Customer Name | Shopify Email | SAP Ariba Buyer ID

3. **Price codes** (if applicable)
   * Determine which price tier each customer belongs to
   * Use consistent naming (e.g., "TIER\_1", "TIER\_2", "VIP")

### Step 2: Access Shopify Admin

### Step 3: Add Tags to Customer

### Step 4: Verify Tags

After saving, verify the tags appear correctly:

1. **Check tag list**: Tags should appear in the customer's profile
2. **Check formatting**: Verify the buyer ID is exact (no typos)
3. **Check uniqueness**: Ensure no other customer has the same buyer ID tag

## Tag Management Best Practices

### Buyer ID Uniqueness

**Rule**: Each buyer ID should be unique to one Shopify customer.

**What happens if duplicate**:

* The system may return the first matching customer
* Pricing may be inconsistent
* Error logged in system

**Best practice**:

* Maintain a master spreadsheet of buyer ID → customer mappings
* Audit tags periodically to check for duplicates
* Remove old tags when buyer IDs change

### Price Code Sharing

**Rule**: Multiple customers can share the same price code.

**Example scenario**:

Customer A: Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352, PRICE CODE: TIER_1
Customer B: Ariba Buyer: AN01631249353, PRICE CODE: TIER_1
Customer C: Ariba Buyer: AN01631249354, PRICE CODE: TIER_2

Customers A and B see the same tier pricing, Customer C sees different pricing.

### Tag Naming Conventions

For consistency across your customer base:

**Buyer ID tags** - Use consistent prefix:

* ✅ Always use "Ariba Buyer:" (recommended)
* ⚠️ Don't mix prefixes randomly (confusing for audits)

**Price code tags** - Use consistent format:

* ✅ "PRICE CODE: TIER\_1", "PRICE CODE: TIER\_2"
* ✅ Use underscores instead of spaces in code names
* ❌ Avoid special characters like @, #, $

**Example good naming**:

Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
PRICE CODE: CORPORATE_A
PRICE CODE: REGION_WEST

ariba AN01631249352      (missing prefix)
Price: Corporate-A%      (inconsistent format, special char)

## Bulk Tagging

### Using Shopify Bulk Editor

For tagging many customers at once:

<Note type="warning">
  **CSV Formatting**: Be careful with commas in tags. If your buyer ID or price code contains a comma, you'll need to escape it properly or use quotes in the CSV.
</Note>

### Using Shopify API (Advanced)

For developers or automation:

See Shopify's [Customer API documentation](https://shopify.dev/api/admin-graphql/latest/mutations/customerUpdate) for details.

## Testing the Configuration

After tagging customers, test that the integration works:

### Method 1: Use PunchOut Simulator

1. **Access simulator**: [PunchOut Cloud Simulator](https://pcsf.cloud.punchoutexpress.com/simulator/cart/cxml.php)
2. **Enter your store URL** with test buyer ID:

3. **Open in browser** and verify custom pricing appears

### Method 2: Manual URL Testing

### Method 3: Check sessionStorage

In browser developer tools:

## Common Configuration Issues

### Issue: Pricing Not Resolving

**Symptoms**: Buyer sees public pricing instead of custom pricing

**Possible causes**:

1. **Customer not tagged**: Verify tag exists in Shopify customer profile
2. **Typo in buyer ID**: Double-check buyer ID matches exactly (case-sensitive for ID part)
3. **Wrong tag format**: Ensure tag starts with approved prefix
4. **Duplicate buyer IDs**: Check if multiple customers have same buyer ID tag

**Solution**:

1. Go to Shopify Admin → Customers
2. Search for customer by buyer ID (use tag search)
3. Verify tag format:

4. Correct any typos or formatting issues
5. Save and test again

### Issue: Multiple Customers Match

**Symptoms**: Error in logs about multiple matching customers

**Cause**: Two or more Shopify customers have the same buyer ID tag

**Solution**:

1. Search Shopify customers for the duplicate buyer ID
2. Determine which customer is correct
3. Remove the buyer ID tag from incorrect customer(s)
4. Consider archiving old/inactive customer accounts

### Issue: Price Code Not Applied

**Symptoms**: Customer pricing works but price code pricing doesn't

**Possible causes**:

1. **Price code tag missing**: Customer has buyer ID tag but no price code tag
2. **Price code not configured**: Price code exists in tag but not in pricing system
3. **Typo in price code**: Price code tag doesn't match pricing system

**Solution**:

1. Verify customer has both tags: buyer ID AND price code
2. Check price code spelling matches pricing system exactly
3. Confirm price code is active in your pricing configuration

### Issue: Tags Not Saving

**Symptoms**: Tags disappear after saving customer profile

**Possible causes**:

1. **Shopify permissions issue**: User account lacks customer edit permissions
2. **Tag validation error**: Tag contains invalid characters
3. **Browser issue**: Cache or session problem

**Solution**:

1. Verify you have "Customers" permission in Shopify Admin
2. Remove any special characters from tags
3. Try a different browser or clear browser cache
4. Contact Shopify support if problem persists

## Updating and Managing Tags

### Changing Buyer IDs

If a customer's SAP Ariba buyer ID changes:

<Note type="info">
  **Backend cache**: It may take up to 15 minutes for the change to take effect due to backend caching. Test after 15 minutes if pricing doesn't update immediately.
</Note>

### Removing PunchOut Access

To disable PunchOut pricing for a customer:

1. Open customer profile in Shopify Admin
2. Remove the buyer ID tag (e.g.,

)
3. Optionally remove price code tag
4. Save changes

The customer will no longer see custom pricing via PunchOut (but can still log in normally if they have a Shopify account).

### Archiving Old Customers

When a B2B customer relationship ends:

**Option 1: Remove Tags Only**

* Remove buyer ID and price code tags
* Keep customer account active (for historical orders)

**Option 2: Archive Customer**

* Remove tags first
* Disable or archive the customer account in Shopify
* Maintains order history but prevents new purchases

## Tag Audit Checklist

Perform regular tag audits (monthly or quarterly):

* \[ ] **Check for duplicates**: Search for duplicate buyer ID tags
* \[ ] **Verify active customers**: Ensure all active B2B customers are tagged
* \[ ] **Remove inactive tags**: Clean up tags from old/inactive customers
* \[ ] **Standardize formats**: Ensure consistent tag formatting across all customers
* \[ ] **Test sample customers**: Verify pricing resolution works for 5-10 random customers
* \[ ] **Update documentation**: Maintain spreadsheet of buyer ID → customer mappings
* \[ ] **Review price codes**: Verify price codes match current pricing structure

## Integration with Other Systems

### Syncing with ERP/CRM

If you use an ERP or CRM system:

**Best practice**:

* Maintain buyer ID mappings in your ERP/CRM
* Use Shopify API to automatically sync tags
* Set up periodic sync (daily or weekly)
* Log sync results for auditing

**Example workflow**:

1. ERP contains: Customer → SAP Ariba Buyer ID → Price Tier
2. Nightly script queries ERP for B2B customers
3. Script updates Shopify customer tags via API
4. Script logs any errors or mismatches

### SAP Ariba Integration

**Coordinate with procurement team**:

* Request list of active buyer IDs from SAP Ariba admin
* Confirm buyer ID format (usually starts with "AN")
* Set up process for new buyer ID notifications
* Establish escalation path for mismatches

## Security Considerations

### Access Control

**Who should have tagging permissions**:

* ✅ Shopify admin with customer management role
* ✅ B2B account manager
* ✅ Customer service team lead

**Who should NOT have tagging permissions**:

* ❌ General customer service reps (risk of errors)
* ❌ External contractors (unless supervised)

### Tag Confidentiality

**Buyer IDs are semi-sensitive**:

* Not personally identifiable information
* But could be used to impersonate buyer in PunchOut
* Don't publish buyer IDs on public websites
* Limit access to spreadsheets containing mappings

### Audit Logging

**Recommended practices**:

* Enable Shopify audit logs (if available in your plan)
* Track who adds/removes buyer ID tags
* Log tag changes in external system
* Review changes quarterly

## Advanced Configurations

### Multiple Buyer IDs per Customer

**Scenario**: One Shopify customer has multiple SAP Ariba buyer IDs.

**Solution**: Add multiple buyer ID tags:

Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
Ariba Buyer: AN01631249353
PRICE CODE: TIER_1

Both buyer IDs will resolve to the same Shopify customer.

### Temporary Price Codes

**Scenario**: Customer gets temporary promotional pricing.

**Solution**:

1. Add temporary price code tag:

2. Configure pricing system for the promo code
3. After promo ends, change tag to:

### Regional Pricing

**Scenario**: Same customer sees different pricing based on ship-to region.

**Solution**: Use compound price codes:

Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
PRICE CODE: TIER_1_US_WEST

title: PunchOut Buyer Guide
description: Guide for procurement buyers using SAP Ariba PunchOut to browse and purchase with custom pricing.
content:
This guide explains how PunchOut buyers experience custom pricing when browsing products through SAP Ariba or other procurement systems.

## What to Expect

When you access a supplier's Shopify store through your SAP Ariba PunchOut catalog, you'll automatically see **your negotiated pricing** without needing to log in or create an account.

### How It Works for You

<Note type="success">
  **No Login Required**: You don't need to create a Shopify account or remember any passwords. Your identity is automatically recognized when you click through from your procurement system.
</Note>

## Recognizing Your Custom Pricing

### How to Tell You're Seeing Custom Pricing

When your custom pricing is active:

* **Prices match your contract**: Products show the negotiated prices you expect
* **Consistent across products**: All products display your customer-specific pricing
* **Same as cart transfer**: Prices match what you'll see when you transfer your cart back to SAP Ariba

### Visual Indicators

Most stores will display pricing normally, but you may notice:

* Product prices that differ from the store's public pricing (if you've seen them before)
* Special price labels or badges indicating customer pricing (store-dependent)
* Your company name or customer ID may appear in the header (store-dependent)

## Browsing Experience

### Viewing Products

**Your custom pricing applies to**:

* Individual product pages
* Collection/category pages (if prices are shown)
* Search results
* Related products and recommendations

**Navigation**:

* Use the store's navigation menu to browse categories
* Use search to find specific products
* Click product links from collections or search results
* Your pricing persists across all pages

### Session Duration

Your custom pricing session:

* **Starts**: When you click through from SAP Ariba
* **Duration**: Lasts up to **12 hours**
* **Ends**: When you close the browser tab OR after 12 hours

### Multiple Browser Tabs

If you open multiple products in different browser tabs:

**Each tab maintains its own session**:

* Tab 1: Your pricing (clicked from SAP Ariba)
* Tab 2: Your pricing (opened from Tab 1)
* Tab 3: Public pricing (if opened directly without PunchOut)

**Best Practice**: Open new tabs by clicking links within the store to maintain your pricing session.

## Shopping & Checkout

### Adding Items to Cart

When you add products to your cart:

1. **Cart shows custom pricing**: Your negotiated prices are maintained
2. **Totals are accurate**: Subtotals and totals reflect your prices
3. **No surprises**: What you see is what you'll pay

### Standard PunchOut Cart Transfer

After adding items to your cart, you can:

**Option 1: Transfer Cart to SAP Ariba** (Traditional PunchOut)

1. Click the "Transfer Cart" or "Punch Back" button
2. Your cart is sent back to SAP Ariba with all items
3. Complete the purchase order in SAP Ariba
4. Your pricing remains consistent

**Option 2: Direct Checkout** (If enabled)

1. Proceed to Shopify checkout
2. Complete payment through the store
3. Pricing matches what you saw while browsing

## Common Scenarios

### Scenario 1: First-Time PunchOut User

**Situation**: You're new to using PunchOut with this supplier.

**What to do**:

1. Log into your SAP Ariba account (or procurement system)
2. Navigate to your supplier's catalog
3. Browse and click on any product
4. The supplier's store opens in a new window/tab
5. Verify you see reasonable pricing (not $0 or blank)
6. If pricing looks wrong, contact your supplier's support

### Scenario 2: Pricing Looks Wrong

**Situation**: You see prices that don't match your contract.

**Possible reasons**:

* You didn't click through from SAP Ariba (opened store directly)
* Your session expired (> 12 hours)
* Your customer account isn't tagged correctly in Shopify
* Technical issue with the integration

**What to do**:

1. Close the browser tab
2. Go back to SAP Ariba
3. Click the product link again
4. If pricing still looks wrong, contact supplier support with:
   * Your SAP Ariba buyer ID
   * Screenshot of the pricing issue
   * Date and time you accessed the store

### Scenario 3: Multiple Buyers on Same Computer

**Situation**: Multiple people in your organization need to browse from the same computer.

**What to do**:

1. **Each buyer uses a separate browser tab**
2. Each person clicks through from their own SAP Ariba session
3. Close your tab when done (to prevent others from seeing your pricing)

**Important**: Closing the tab ensures your pricing doesn't carry over to the next person.

### Scenario 4: Session Expired

**Situation**: You left a tab open overnight and now see different prices.

**What to do**:

1. Close the expired tab
2. Return to SAP Ariba
3. Click through again to start a fresh session
4. Your custom pricing will be restored

## Privacy & Security

### What Information Is Stored?

When you browse with PunchOut:

**Stored locally (in your browser)**:

* Your SAP Ariba buyer ID (e.g., "AN01631249352")
* Timestamp of when you clicked through

**NOT stored**:

* No personal information
* No browsing history
* No credit card details
* No passwords

### How Long Is Data Kept?

* **Browser session**: Up to 12 hours
* **After closing tab**: Immediately cleared
* **On supplier servers**: Resolution cached for 15 minutes

### Multiple Buyers on Shared Computer

**Privacy protection**:

* Each browser tab has isolated storage
* Closing your tab clears your buyer ID
* Other users can't see your pricing in new tabs

**Best practice**: Always close your browser tab when finished to prevent others from accessing your pricing session.

## Troubleshooting for Buyers

### I See "$0.00" or Blank Pricing

**Possible causes**:

* Supplier hasn't configured pricing for you yet
* Product is out of stock
* Technical issue with widget

**Solution**:

* Contact supplier support
* Provide your SAP Ariba buyer ID
* Mention specific products with pricing issues

### Prices Changed While Browsing

**Possible causes**:

* Your session expired (> 12 hours)
* Browser tab was refreshed without fromIdentity parameter
* You navigated to a page outside the PunchOut session

**Solution**:

* Close tab and click through from SAP Ariba again
* Verify URL includes your buyer ID parameter
* Contact support if problem persists

### "Access Denied" or Error Messages

**Possible causes**:

* Your customer account isn't set up in Shopify
* Store configuration issue
* Your buyer ID isn't recognized

**Solution**:

* Contact supplier support immediately
* Provide your SAP Ariba buyer ID
* Include screenshot of error message

### Pricing Doesn't Match Purchase Order

**Possible causes**:

* Pricing changed between browsing and cart transfer
* Product has time-sensitive pricing
* Different pricing rules in SAP Ariba vs. Shopify

**Solution**:

* Review your purchase order in SAP Ariba before approving
* Contact supplier if there's a significant discrepancy
* Pricing in SAP Ariba is always the final price

### Cart Transfer Fails

**Note**: This is a separate feature from fromIdentity pricing.

**Solution**:

* See supplier's PunchOut cart transfer documentation
* Contact supplier support for cart transfer issues
* Provide order details and error messages

## Tips for Best Experience

### Before You Start

* Ensure you're logged into SAP Ariba or your procurement system
* Use a modern web browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
* Allow pop-ups from SAP Ariba (for opening store links)

### While Browsing

* Always click through from SAP Ariba for first product
* Open new products by clicking links within the store
* Don't bookmark product pages (bookmarks won't include your buyer ID)
* Check pricing on first product to confirm it's working

### When Finished

* Close the browser tab when you're done shopping
* Don't leave tabs open overnight (session expires)
* Transfer cart to SAP Ariba before closing (if you haven't checked out)

### For Teams

* Each team member should click through from their own SAP Ariba account
* Don't share browser tabs between buyers
* Close tabs to prevent pricing confusion for other users

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Do I need to create a Shopify account?

**No.** Your SAP Ariba buyer ID automatically identifies you and shows your custom pricing. You don't need to create an account or log in.

### Can I bookmark products to view later?

**Not recommended.** Bookmarks won't include your buyer ID parameter, so bookmarked pages will show public pricing instead of your custom pricing. Always access products through SAP Ariba for accurate pricing.

### How long does my pricing session last?

**12 hours** from when you first click through from SAP Ariba. After 12 hours, you'll need to click through again to refresh your session.

### Can I browse on my phone?

**Yes**, if your procurement system supports mobile access. Click through from SAP Ariba on your mobile device just like you would on desktop. The same 12-hour session rules apply.

### Will my pricing work across all suppliers?

**It depends.** Each supplier must implement the fromIdentity pricing feature. Contact your suppliers to ask if they support PunchOut custom pricing during browsing.

### What if I see different prices in SAP Ariba vs. the store?

This shouldn't happen if the integration is working correctly. The prices you see while browsing should match the prices in your cart transfer. If there's a discrepancy:

1. Take screenshots of both prices
2. Note the product SKU or name
3. Contact supplier support

### Can I use discount codes or coupons?

**Check with your supplier.** Some suppliers may allow additional discounts on top of your custom pricing, while others may restrict coupon usage for B2B customers.

### Is my browsing data tracked?

**No.** The feature only stores your buyer ID locally in your browser to display correct pricing. No browsing history or personal data is tracked or stored beyond standard e-commerce analytics.

## Getting Help

### Contact Your Supplier

If you experience issues with pricing or PunchOut integration:

**Information to provide**:

* Your SAP Ariba buyer ID (e.g., "AN01631249352")
* Product(s) with incorrect pricing
* Screenshots showing the issue
* Date and time you accessed the store
* Browser and device you're using

### Contact Your Procurement Team

For questions about:

* Your SAP Ariba account or buyer ID
* Approved suppliers and catalogs
* Purchase order workflows
* Procurement policies

Contact your organization's procurement department.

## Related Resources

<Note type="info">
  This guide is for **buyers and procurement users**. If you're a **store administrator** or **developer**, see the [Admin Setup Guide](/docs/punchout/admin-setup) or [Deployment Guide](/docs/punchout/deployment).
</Note>

title: PunchOut Deployment Guide
description: Install the fromIdentity tracker script and deploy the Product Page Widget to enable PunchOut custom pricing.
content: This guide explains how to deploy the PunchOut fromIdentity customer pricing feature, including tracker script installation and widget deployment. This is intended for developers, technical administrators, and implementation teams. ## Overview Deploying PunchOut custom pricing requires two main components: 1. **Tracker Script**: Captures the `fromIdentity` parameter from URLs and stores it in sessionStorage 2. **Product Page Widget**: Reads fromIdentity and displays customer-specific pricing Both components must be deployed for the feature to work correctly. ## Prerequisites Before you begin: * \[ ] Access to Shopify theme editor * \[ ] Weevio Product Page Widget source code * \[ ] Node.js 22.x installed (for building widget) * \[ ] Understanding of Shopify Liquid templates * \[ ] Customer tagging configured (see [Admin Setup Guide](/docs/punchout/admin-setup)) ## Deployment Architecture ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Shopify Theme (theme.liquid) │ │ ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ... ``` ### Step 4: Verify Tracker Installation If you see the console log and sessionStorage contains the data, the tracker script is installed correctly! ### Step 5: Test Cross-Page Persistence The fromIdentity value should persist across all pages in the same browser tab until the tab is closed or 12 hours have passed. ## Part 2: Product Page Widget Deployment ### Step 1: Build Updated Widget ### Step 2: Deploy Widget to Hosting The widget deployment process depends on your hosting setup: **Option A: Shopify Theme Assets** **Option B: CDN Hosting** **Standard Deployment**: Follow your existing widget deployment process. The fromIdentity feature is automatically included if you're using the latest widget version—no special configuration needed. ### Step 3: Widget Configuration Ensure your widget initialization includes the required configuration: ```javascript // Widget initialization (usually in product template) window.WeevioWidget.init({ targetElement: '#weevio-widget', shopifyDomain: 'yourstore.myshopify.com', apiEndpoint: 'https://api.weevio.com', // fromIdentity support is automatic // No additional config needed }); ``` The widget automatically: 1. Checks sessionStorage for `weevio_fromIdentity` 2. Calls `/api/v1/shopify/b2b/resolve-from-identity` if found 3. Fetches customer-specific pricing 4. Falls back to public pricing if resolution fails ## Testing the Complete Integration ### Test 1: End-to-End Pricing Resolution ### Test 2: Fallback Behavior ### Test 3: Session Expiration ### Test 4: widget init * Ensure backend API is deployed and accessible * Check for CORS issues in Network tab ### Issue: Session Not Persisting Across Pages **Symptoms**: * fromIdentity captured on first page * are clearing sessionStorage ### Issue: Pricing Updates Delayed **Symptoms**: * Customer tag changed in Shopify * Widget still shows old pricing after 1-2 minutes **Cause**: Backend caching (15-minute calling resolution API * Network request to `/resolve-from-identity` is blocked **Cause**: API endpoint not configured to accept requests from your Shopify domain **Solution**: * Verify backend API CORS configuration includes your Shopify store domain * Check that API requests use correct domain and protocol (HTTPS) * Contact Weevio backend team if CORS configuration needs updating ## Performance Optimization ### Caching Strategy The fromIdentity feature uses two levels of caching: **Backend Cache (15 minutes)**: * Resolution API results cached for 15 minutes * Reduces Shopify API calls * Automatic cache expiration **Frontend Cache (12 hours)**: * sessionStorage persists fromIdentity for 12 hours * Eliminates repeated parameter parsing * Automatic expiration on tab close ### [Weevio Widget] Displaying customer pricing ``` **Error logs**: ``` [Weevio Widget] Resolution failed: Customer not found [Weevio Widget] Falling back to public pricing ``` ### Production Logging In production, consider: **Logging successful resolutions**: * Track how many buyers use fromIdentity feature * Monitor resolution success rate * Identify most active buyer IDs **Logging errors**: * Log resolution failures (customer not found) * Track expired sessions * Monitor API errors **Analytics integration**: * Send events to Google Analytics or similar * Track "fromIdentity\_captured" event * Track "pricing\_resolved" or "pricing\_fallback" events ### Maintenance Checklist Perform these tasks regularly: **Weekly**: * \[ ] Check error logs for resolution failures * \[ ] Verify tracker script still loading correctly * \[ ] Test with a few real buyer IDs **Monthly**: * \[ ] Review customer tag audit (see [Admin Setup Guide](/docs/punchout/admin-setup)) * \[ ] Check for duplicate buyer ID tags * \[ ] Verify pricing data is accurate **Quarterly**: * \[ ] Review bundle size and performance metrics * \[ ] Test on new browser versions * \[ ] Update documentation for any changes ## Next Steps ## Support For deployment assistance or technical issues: **Contact Weevio Development Team** with: * Shopify store domain * Widget version number * Browser console logs (screenshots or copy/paste) * Network tab screenshot showing API requests * Description of issue and steps to reproduce **Include deployment details**: * When you deployed (date/time) * Which components were deployed (tracker, widget, both) * Testing results from this guide * Any error messages or warnings

title: PunchOut Integration
description: Enable SAP Ariba buyers to see customer-specific pricing automatically without Shopify login.
content:
PunchOut integration enables your SAP Ariba buyers to see their negotiated, customer-specific pricing automatically when browsing your Shopify store—without requiring them to log in.

## Overview

The PunchOut fromIdentity customer pricing feature creates a seamless experience for B2B procurement buyers. When a buyer accesses your store through their SAP Ariba PunchOut catalog, the system automatically identifies them and displays their contracted pricing on all products.

### How It Works

The integration uses a three-part system:

1. **URL Parameter Capture**: When a buyer clicks through from SAP Ariba, a `fromIdentity` parameter (their SAP Ariba buyer ID) is included in the URL
2. **Customer Mapping**: The system maps this buyer ID to a Shopify customer record using special tags
3. **Automatic Pricing**: The Product Page Widget automatically displays the customer's negotiated pricing

**Example Flow**:

```
Buyer clicks product in SAP Ariba
  ↓
Opens: yourstore.com?fromIdentity=AN01631249352
  ↓
System looks up Shopify customer tagged: "Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352"
  ↓
Displays customer-specific pricing automatically
```

### Key Benefits

## Core Components

### Backend API

* **Endpoint**: `/api/v1/shopify/b2b/resolve-from-identity`
* **Purpose**: Maps SAP Ariba buyer IDs to Shopify customer IDs
* **Caching**: 15-minute HTTP cache for performance
* **Security**: Input sanitization and validation at all layers

### Tracker Script

* **Size**: 1.47 KB (0.66 KB gzipped)
* **Function**: Captures fromIdentity parameter from any page URL
* **Storage**: Stores in sessionStorage with 12-hour expiration
* **Installation**: Added once to theme.liquid

### Widget Integration

* **Component**: Product Page Widget (PriceDisplay)
* **Function**: Automatically queries and displays customer pricing
* **Fallback**: Shows public pricing if resolution fails

## Who This Is For

## Technical Highlights

### Session Management

* **Storage**: Browser sessionStorage (per-tab isolation)
* **Duration**: 12-hour automatic expiration
* **Privacy**: No cookies, no cross-site tracking
* **Multi-buyer**: Different tabs can have different buyer contexts

### Performance

* **Two-level caching**: Backend (15 min) + Frontend (session-based)
* **Graceful degradation**: Falls back to public pricing on any error
* **Minimal overhead**: ~4.5 KB total bundle impact
* **Real-time pricing**: Updates immediately when products are viewed

### Customer Tagging

Shopify customers must be tagged with their SAP Ariba buyer ID using one of these formats:

```
Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
SAP Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
PunchOut Buyer: AN01631249352
```

Optional price code tag for tiered pricing:

```
PRICE CODE: IPG_TIER_1
```

## Getting Started

Ready to implement PunchOut pricing? Follow this sequence:

## Feature Compatibility

### Requirements

* Weevio Product Page Widget (with PunchOut support)
* Shopify store with customer accounts
* Customer tagging capability in Shopify
* Modern web browser with sessionStorage support

### Browser Support

* Chrome 4+
* Firefox 3.5+
* Safari 4+
* Edge (all versions)
* Mobile browsers (iOS Safari, Chrome Mobile)

## Documentation Structure

## Support & Questions

For technical implementation questions or issues with the PunchOut integration, consult the [Troubleshooting Guide](/docs/punchout/troubleshooting) or contact the Weevio Cloud development team.

title: PunchOut Overview
description: Detailed technical overview of the PunchOut fromIdentity customer pricing integration.
content:
The PunchOut fromIdentity customer pricing feature enables SAP Ariba buyers to see their negotiated pricing automatically when browsing your Shopify store, without requiring them to log in or create a Shopify account.

## What Is PunchOut?

**PunchOut** is a procurement protocol that allows buyers to "punch out" from their procurement system (like SAP Ariba) to browse a supplier's catalog with their specific pricing, then "punch back" to their procurement system with selected items.

Traditional PunchOut implementations only handle the final cart transfer. This feature extends PunchOut by providing **real-time custom pricing throughout the browsing experience**.

## The fromIdentity Parameter

### What Is fromIdentity?

The `fromIdentity` parameter is a unique identifier sent by SAP Ariba (and other procurement systems) that represents the buyer's identity. This is typically the buyer's SAP Ariba buyer ID.

**Example**:

```
https://yourstore.com/products/widget?fromIdentity=AN01631249352
```

When a buyer clicks a product link in their SAP Ariba catalog, this parameter is automatically included in the URL.

### How It Works

## Architecture Components

### 1. Tracker Script (Frontend)

**File**: `fromIdentity-tracker.js`\
**Size**: 1.47 KB (0.66 KB gzipped)\
**Location**: Shopify theme assets

**Responsibilities**:

* Runs on every page load
* Checks URL for `fromIdentity` parameter
* Stores value in sessionStorage with 12-hour TTL
* Provides console logging for debugging

**Example Code Flow**:

```javascript
// On page load
if (urlParams.has('fromIdentity')) {
  const fromIdentity = urlParams.get('fromIdentity');
  sessionStorage.setItem('weevio_fromIdentity', JSON.stringify({
    value: fromIdentity,
    timestamp: Date.now()
  }));
  console.log('[Weevio] Captured fromIdentity:', fromIdentity);
}
```

### 2. Session Manager (Frontend)

**Module**: `fromIdentityManager.ts`\
**Location**: Widget codebase

**Responsibilities**:

* Reads fromIdentity from sessionStorage
* Validates timestamp (12-hour expiration)
* Provides clean API for widget components
* Handles edge cases (expired sessions, invalid data)

**API**:

```typescript
// Get current fromIdentity (if valid)
const fromIdentity = getFromIdentity(); // Returns string | null

// Check if fromIdentity exists and is valid
const isValid = hasValidFromIdentity(); // Returns boolean
```

### 3. Backend Resolution API

**Endpoint**: `GET /api/v1/shopify/b2b/resolve-from-identity`

**Query Parameters**:

* `fromIdentity` (required): SAP Ariba buyer ID
* `domain` (required): Shopify store domain
* `teamID` (optional): Weevio team identifier

**Response (Success)**:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "customerId": "gid://shopify/Customer/7234567890",
    "priceCode": "IPG_TIER_1"
  }
}
```

**Response (Not Found)**:

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "No customer found with fromIdentity"
}
```

**Caching**: 15 minutes (Cache-Control header)

### 4. Product Page Widget Integration

**Component**: `PriceDisplay.tsx`

**Responsibilities**:

* Checks for fromIdentity in sessionStorage
* Calls resolution API if fromIdentity exists
* Fetches customer-specific pricing
* Displays pricing (or falls back to public pricing)
* Handles errors gracefully

**Pricing Resolution Flow**:

```
1. Widget loads on product page
2. Check sessionStorage for fromIdentity
3. If found: Call /resolve-from-identity API
4. If successful: Fetch customer pricing
5. Display customer pricing
6. If any step fails: Display public pricing
```

## Data Flow Diagram

```
┌─────────────────┐
│   SAP Ariba     │
│  Buyer clicks   │
│    product      │
└────────┬────────┘
         │ URL with ?fromIdentity=AN01631249352
         ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         Shopify Store (Any Page)            │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │    fromIdentity Tracker Script        │  │
│  │  • Detects fromIdentity in URL        │  │
│  │  • Saves to sessionStorage            │  │
│  │  • Sets 12-hour expiration            │  │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────┘  │
└──────────────────┬──────────────────────────┘
                   │ sessionStorage: weevio_fromIdentity
                   ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│       Product Page Widget Loads             │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │    fromIdentity Manager               │  │
│  │  • Reads from sessionStorage          │  │
│  │  • Validates timestamp (< 12 hours)   │  │
│  └───────────┬───────────────────────────┘  │
│              │ fromIdentity value           │
│              ↓                               │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │     API Call to Backend               │  │
│  │  /resolve-from-identity               │  │
│  │  ?fromIdentity=AN01631249352          │  │
│  └───────────┬───────────────────────────┘  │
└──────────────┼──────────────────────────────┘
               │
               ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│         Weevio Backend API                  │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │   B2B Controller                      │  │
│  │  • Queries Shopify GraphQL API        │  │
│  │  • Searches for customer with tag:    │  │
│  │    "Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352"       │  │
│  │  • Returns customerId + priceCode     │  │
│  └───────────┬───────────────────────────┘  │

               │ { customerId, priceCode }
               ↓

│  │  • Displays negotiated price          │  │

│  └───────────────────────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Customer Tagging System

### Tag Format

Shopify customers must be tagged with their SAP Ariba buyer ID. The system recognizes these tag patterns:

**Accepted Prefixes** (case-insensitive, spaces/underscores flexible):

* `Ariba Buyer:`
* `SAP Ariba Buyer:`
* `PunchOut Buyer:`

**Example Tags**:

```
Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
SAP Ariba Buyer: AN01631249352
PunchOut Buyer: AN01631249352
ariba_buyer:AN01631249352
ARIBA BUYER: AN01631249352
```

**Optional Price Code Tag**:

```
PRICE CODE: IPG_TIER_1
price_code:CORPORATE_DISCOUNT
```

### Tag Resolution Logic

When the backend receives a `fromIdentity` value (e.g., `AN01631249352`), it:

1. **Queries Shopify** for customers with tags matching the patterns above
2. **Extracts** the buyer ID from the tag (part after the colon)
3. **Compares** the extracted ID to the `fromIdentity` parameter
4. **Returns** the matching customer's Shopify ID and price code (if tagged)

**Example Query**:

```graphql
query {
  customers(first: 1, query: "tag:AN01631249352") {
    edges {
      node {
        id
        tags
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Session Persistence

### Storage Method

The feature uses **sessionStorage** (not cookies or localStorage) for privacy and multi-buyer support:

| Feature | sessionStorage | Cookie | localStorage |
|---------|---------------|--------|--------------|
| **Per-tab isolation** | ✅ Yes | ❌ No | ❌ No |
| **Auto-expiration** | ✅ On tab close | ⚠️ Manual | ❌ No |
| **Privacy-friendly** | ✅ No tracking | ❌ Trackable | ⚠️ Persistent |
| **Multi-buyer support** | ✅ Different tabs | ❌ Shared | ❌ Shared |

### Session Lifecycle

### Multi-Tab Behavior

**Scenario**: Two buyers on the same computer, different browser tabs

**Tab 1**: Opens with `?fromIdentity=BUYER_A`\
→ sessionStorage stores `BUYER_A`\
→ Displays pricing for Customer A

**Tab 2**: Opens with `?fromIdentity=BUYER_B`\
→ sessionStorage stores `BUYER_B` (separate tab storage)\
→ Displays pricing for Customer B

Each tab maintains its own isolated session, enabling multiple buyers to browse simultaneously.

## Caching Strategy

### Two-Level Cache

**Level 1: Backend (15 minutes)**

* Caches customer resolution results
* Reduces Shopify API calls
* Improves response time
* HTTP Cache-Control header

**Level 2: Frontend (12 hours)**

* Stores fromIdentity in sessionStorage
* Eliminates repeated parameter parsing
* Maintains session across page navigation
* Automatic expiration

### Cache Invalidation

**Backend Cache (15 min)**:

* Automatically expires after 15 minutes
* Can be manually cleared via admin tools
* New tags or customer updates reflected within 15 minutes

**Frontend Cache (12 hours)**:

* Automatically expires after 12 hours
* Manually cleared by closing the browser tab
* Buyer can refresh by clicking through from SAP Ariba again

## Security Considerations

### Input Validation

**fromIdentity Parameter**:

* Sanitized to prevent XSS attacks
* Limited to alphanumeric characters and standard symbols
* Maximum length enforced
* Invalid values rejected with error response

**API Endpoint**:

* Rate limiting applied (standard API limits)
* Authentication required for backend access
* HTTPS only (no unencrypted transmission)
* CORS policies enforced

### Privacy Protection

**No Tracking**:

* No cookies set
* No cross-site tracking
* No personally identifiable information logged
* sessionStorage cleared on tab close

**Data Minimization**:

* Only stores buyer ID (no personal data)
* No browsing history tracked
* No analytics collection specific to PunchOut users

## Performance Impact

### Bundle Size

* **Tracker Script**: 1.47 KB (0.66 KB gzipped)
* **Session Manager**: ~2 KB (included in widget)
* **Widget Integration**: ~1 KB additional code
* **Total Impact**: ~4.5 KB (minimal)

### Runtime Performance

* Tracker runs once per page load (~1ms)
* Session check is synchronous read from sessionStorage (~0.1ms)
* API call cached on backend (15 min TTL)
* No impact on page load time or rendering

### Scalability

* Backend resolution API can handle 1000+ requests/second
* Caching reduces Shopify API load by 99%
* No database bottlenecks (Shopify handles queries)

## Graceful Degradation

The feature is designed to fail gracefully:

| Failure Scenario | Behavior |
|-----------------|----------|
| Tracker script fails to load | Widget shows public pricing (standard behavior) |
| sessionStorage unavailable | Widget shows public pricing |
| Resolution API timeout | Widget shows public pricing |
| Customer not found | Widget shows public pricing |
| Invalid fromIdentity format | Widget shows public pricing |
| Expired session (> 12 hours) | Widget shows public pricing |

**Result**: Buyers always see pricing (public or custom), never a blank page or error message.

## Comparison with Traditional PunchOut

### Traditional PunchOut (Cart Transfer Only)

**What it does**:

* Buyer browses catalog with **public pricing**
* Adds items to cart
* Clicks "Transfer Cart" to punch back to SAP Ariba
* **SAP Ariba applies custom pricing** when importing cart

**Limitations**:

* Buyer doesn't see their real prices while browsing
* Can cause confusion ("Why does SAP show different prices?")
* Requires buyer to add items to cart to see custom pricing

### Enhanced PunchOut (fromIdentity Feature)

**What it does**:

* Buyer browses catalog with **custom pricing from the start**
* Sees accurate totals before adding to cart
* Cart transfer still works normally
* **Consistent pricing** in store and SAP Ariba

**Benefits**:

* Better buyer experience
* Reduces "cart abandonment" due to price confusion
* More accurate purchasing decisions
* Maintains cart transfer compatibility

## Related Features

This feature complements the existing PunchOut cart transfer functionality. Both features can be used together:

* **fromIdentity pricing** (this feature): Displays custom pricing while browsing
* **Cart transfer**: Sends cart contents back to SAP Ariba for purchase

## Next Steps

title: PunchOut Troubleshooting
description: Diagnose and resolve common issues with PunchOut fromIdentity customer pricing integration.
content:
This guide helps you diagnose and resolve common issues with the PunchOut fromIdentity customer pricing feature. ## Quick Diagnostics Before diving into specific issues, run these quick checks: ## Common Issues & Solutions ### Buyer Sees Public Pricing Instead of Custom Pricing **Symptoms**: * Buyer clicked through from SAP Ariba * Product pages show public pricing * Buyer expects to see custom pricing **Diagnostic Steps**: ### Pricing Works Initially Then Stops Working **Symptoms**: * Custom pricing worked when buyer first visited * After browsing for a while, pricing changed to public * Buyer didn't close the tab **Most Likely Cause**: Session expired (> 12 hours) **Solution**: **Other Possible Causes**: * Browser tab was refreshed by clicking browser refresh (doesn't preserve fromIdentity parameter) * Buyer navigated to a bookmarked page (bookmarks don't include fromIdentity) * sessionStorage was manually cleared (unlikely) ### Different Pricing in Different Browser Tabs **Symptoms**: * Tab 1 shows one set of prices * Tab 2 (opened later) shows different prices * Buyer is confused about which pricing is correct **This is Expected Behavior** sessionStorage is **per-tab isolated** by design. Each tab maintains its own buyer context. **Common Scenarios**: **Scenario A: Two Different Buyers** * Tab 1: Buyer A clicked from SAP Ariba → Shows Buyer A pricing * Tab 2: Buyer B clicked from SAP Ariba → Shows Buyer B pricing * **Result**: Different pricing is correct **Scenario B: Same Buyer, Different Entry Points** * Tab 1: Buyer clicked from SAP Ariba → Shows custom pricing * Tab 2: Buyer opened bookmark or typed URL → Shows public pricing * **Result**: Tab 2 doesn't have fromIdentity, shows public pricing **Solution**: * Educate buyers to always click through from SAP Ariba * Open new products by clicking links within the store (maintains session) * Don't bookmark product pages * Close old tabs when finished browsing ### Tracker Script Issues **Symptoms**: * No console log when visiting page with fromIdentity parameter * sessionStorage doesn't contain weevio\_fromIdentity **Diagnostic Steps**: **Solutions**: 1. **Re-upload tracker script** to Shopify Assets 2. **Clear Shopify theme cache**: Theme editor → Actions → Clear cache 3. **Verify theme.liquid saved correctly** 4. **Test in incognito window** to rule out browser cache issues ### Session Storage Issues **Symptoms**: * Tracker script works (console logs appear) * But sessionStorage doesn't contain fromIdentity * Or sessionStorage data is corrupt **Diagnostic Steps**: **Solutions**: 1. **Use modern browser**: Chrome 4+, Firefox 3.5+, Safari 4+, Edge 2. **Check privacy settings**: Allow sessionStorage (usually enabled by default) 3. **Identify conflicting scripts**: Disable other scripts temporarily to isolate issue ### Customer Tagging Issues **Symptoms**: * Tracker captures fromIdentity correctly * Customer exists in Shopify * But resolution API returns "Customer not found" **Diagnostic Steps**: **Solutions**: 1. **Add missing tags**: Tag customer with correct format 2. **Fix typos**: Correct buyer ID to match exactly 3. **Standardize format**: Use consistent prefix (e.g., always "Ariba Buyer:") 4. **Wait 15 minutes**: Backend cache takes up to 15 minutes to update ### API Resolution Issues **Symptoms**: * Tracker and sessionStorage working * Customer tagged correctly * But API returns errors or doesn't respond **Diagnostic Steps**: **Solutions Based on Status Code**: | Status | Issue | Solution | |--------|-------|----------| | 200 (success: false) | Customer not found | Fix customer tagging | | 400 | Invalid request | Check widget configuration | | 404 | Endpoint not found | Verify API deployment | | 500 | Backend error | Contact Weevio support | | 503 | Service unavailable | Wait and retry | | CORS error | CORS misconfiguration | Contact Weevio backend team | ### Widget Issues **Symptoms**: * Tracker working correctly * Customer tagged correctly * But widget doesn't call resolution API **Diagnostic Steps**: **Solutions**: 1. **Deploy updated widget** with fromIdentity support 2. **Clear browser cache** and hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) 3. **Verify widget initialization** includes correct configuration 4. **Check for JavaScript errors** blocking widget execution ### Resolution Failures **Symptoms**: * API is called * API returns `success: false` * Message: "No customer found with fromIdentity" **This means**: * Customer doesn't exist in Shopify with matching buyer ID tag * OR customer exists but tag format is incorrect * OR multiple customers have same buyer ID (ambiguous match) **Diagnostic Steps**: **Solutions**: 1. **Tag the customer** if not tagged (see [Admin Setup Guide](/docs/punchout/admin-setup)) 2. **Fix tag format** if incorrect 3. **Fix typos** in buyer ID 4. **Remove duplicates** if multiple customers have same buyer ID 5. **Wait 15 minutes** for backend cache to refresh ### Handling Duplicate Buyer IDs **Symptoms**: * Search for buyer ID returns multiple customers * Resolution is inconsistent **Why This Is a Problem**: * System doesn't know which customer to use * May return first match (unpredictable) * Pricing may be wrong **Solution**: ### Session Expiration Issues **Symptoms**: * Buyer browsing for extended period * Pricing suddenly changes to public * Console shows: "Session expired" **This is Expected Behavior**: Sessions expire after 12 hours **Solution**: **Preventing Confusion**: * Document session duration in buyer guide * Consider adding session timeout warning in UI (optional enhancement) * Train support team on session expiration behavior ## Debugging Tools ### Browser Console Commands **Check fromIdentity**: ```javascript // Get fromIdentity data const data = sessionStorage.getItem('weevio_fromIdentity'); console.log(JSON.parse(data)); // Check session age const parsed = JSON.parse(data); const hours = (Date.now() - parsed.timestamp) / (1000 * 60 * 60); console.log('Session age:', hours, 'hours'); // Manually clear session (for testing) sessionStorage.removeItem('weevio_fromIdentity'); // Manually set test data sessionStorage.setItem('weevio_fromIdentity', JSON.stringify({ value: 'TEST_BUYER_123', timestamp: Date.now() })); ``` **Check widget state**: ```javascript // Check if widget loaded typeof window.WeevioWidget !== 'undefined'; // Check widget version (if available) window.WeevioWidget.version; // Check if tracker loaded typeof window.WeevioFromIdentityTracker !== 'undefined'; ``` ### Testing Resolution API Manually **Using Browser Console**: ```javascript // Test resolution API fetch('/api/v1/shopify/b2b/resolve-from-identity?fromIdentity=AN01631249352&domain=yourstore.myshopify.com') .then(res => res.json()) .then(data => console.log(data)) .catch(err => console.error(err)); ``` **Using cURL**: ```bash curl "https://api.weevio.com/api/v1/shopify/b2b/resolve-from-identity?fromIdentity=AN01631249352&domain=yourstore.myshopify.com" ``` **Expected Response (Success)**: ```json { "success": true, "data": { "customerId": "gid://shopify/Customer/7234567890", "priceCode": "IPG_TIER_1" } } ``` **Expected Response (Not Found)**: ```json { "success": false, "message": "No customer found with fromIdentity" } ``` ### Network Analysis **In DevTools → Network tab**: 1. **Filter for API requests**: Type "resolve" in filter box 2. **Check request details**: * Request URL (verify fromIdentity and domain parameters) * Request headers (verify authorization if applicable) * Status code (200, 400, 404, 500, etc.) 3. **Check response**: * Response body (JSON with success/data) * Response headers (Cache-Control, CORS headers) * Timing (should be \< 100ms with cache) **Check timing**: * **Cached response**: \< 50ms (backend cache hit) * **Fresh query**: 100-500ms (Shopify API query) * **Timeout**: > 5000ms (network or backend issue) ## Performance Issues ### Slow Pricing Resolution **Symptoms**: * Pricing takes several seconds to appear * Widget shows loading state for extended time **Possible Causes**: 1. **Slow network**: Check network speed 2. **Backend not caching**: Should see 15-minute cache headers 3. **Shopify API slow**: Check Shopify status 4. **Large customer base**: Many customers slows GraphQL query **Diagnostic Steps**: **Solutions**: 1. **Verify backend caching** is enabled (15-minute TTL) 2. **Check Shopify API status**: [status.shopify.com](https://www.shopify.com/admin/settings/general) 3. **Optimize customer query**: Ensure Shopify customer tags are indexed 4. **Contact Weevio support**: If persistent slow performance ### Widget Not Loading **Symptoms**: * Product page renders but no pricing widget appears * No widget logs in console **Diagnostic Steps**: **Solutions**: 1. **Add widget container** to product template: `
` 2. **Deploy widget script** (see [Deployment Guide](/docs/punchout/deployment)) 3. **Fix JavaScript errors** blocking widget initialization 4. **Verify widget initialization code** is present and correct ## Error Messages Reference ### Console Error Messages | Error Message | Meaning | Solution | |--------------|---------|----------| | `[Weevio] Failed to capture fromIdentity` | Tracker script error | Check tracker script installation | | `[Weevio Widget] sessionStorage not available` | Browser doesn't support sessionStorage | Use modern browser | | `[Weevio Widget] fromIdentity expired` | Session > 12 hours old | Refresh session by clicking from SAP Ariba | | `[Weevio Widget] Resolution API failed` | API error or timeout | Check network, verify API endpoint | | `[Weevio Widget] Customer not found` | No matching customer tag | Tag customer correctly | | `[Weevio Widget] Multiple customers found` | Duplicate buyer IDs | Remove duplicate tags | | `[Weevio Widget] Falling back to public pricing` | Resolution failed, showing default | Check previous errors for root cause | ### API Error Responses | Response | Meaning | Action | |----------|---------|--------| | `success: false, message: "No customer found"` | Customer not tagged | Add/fix customer tag | | `success: false, message: "Multiple customers found"` | Duplicate buyer IDs | Remove duplicates | | `success: false, message: "Invalid fromIdentity"` | Malformed buyer ID | Check parameter format | | Status 400 | Bad request | Check API parameters | | Status 404 | Endpoint not found | Verify API deployment | | Status 500 | Internal server error | Contact Weevio support | | Status 503 | Service unavailable | Wait and retry later | ## Support Escalation ### When to Contact Weevio Support Contact support if: * \[ ] You've followed all troubleshooting steps * \[ ] Issue persists across multiple buyers * \[ ] Backend API is returning errors * \[ ] Performance is consistently slow (> 1 second) * \[ ] You suspect a bug in the feature ### Information to Provide When contacting support, include: **Environment Details**: * Shopify store domain * Widget version number * Browser and version * Operating system **Issue Details**: * Buyer ID (fromIdentity value) * Specific product URL * Expected behavior vs. actual behavior * When issue started (date/time) **Diagnostic Data**: * Browser console logs (screenshot or copy/paste) * Network tab screenshot (showing API request/response) * sessionStorage value (from console) * Customer tags in Shopify (screenshot) **Steps to Reproduce**: 1. Detailed steps to trigger the issue 2. Whether issue is consistent or intermittent 3. Whether issue affects all buyers or specific buyer(s) ### Before Contacting Support Complete this checklist: * \[ ] Verified tracker script is installed and loading * \[ ] Confirmed sessionStorage contains fromIdentity * \[ ] Checked customer is tagged correctly in Shopify * \[ ] Tested API resolution manually * \[ ] Cleared browser cache and retried * \[ ] Tested in different browser * \[ ] Waited 15 minutes for backend cache to update (if tag recently added) * \[ ] Reviewed all sections of this troubleshooting guide ## Prevention & Best Practices ### Regular Maintenance **Weekly**: * Monitor error logs for resolution failures * Check for new buyer IDs that need tagging **Monthly**: * Audit customer tags for accuracy * Remove old/inactive buyer ID tags * Test with sample buyer IDs **Quarterly**: * Review performance metrics * Update documentation for any changes * Train new support staff on troubleshooting ### Buyer Education **Share with procurement teams**: * [Buyer Guide](/docs/punchout/buyer-guide) - explains their experience * Session duration (12 hours) * Importance of clicking through from SAP Ariba * What to do if pricing looks wrong ### Support Team Training **Train support staff on**: * How to verify tracker script installation * How to check browser console logs * How to verify customer tagging * When to escalate to technical team ### Documentation **Maintain**: * Spreadsheet of buyer ID → customer mappings * Deployment history (when deployed, which version) * Known issues and workarounds * Support ticket history for patterns ## Related Resources **Can't find your issue?** Contact Weevio Support with detailed diagnostic information from this guide. Include console logs, network traces, and steps to reproduce.

keywords: Scenario B: Same Buyer, Different Entry Points

### Testing Resolution API Manually

**Using Browser Console**:

### Network Analysis

**In DevTools → Network tab**:

1. **Filter for API requests**: Type "resolve" in filter box
2. **Check request details**:
   * Request URL (verify fromIdentity and domain parameters)
   * Request headers (verify authorization if applicable)
   * Status code (200, 400, 404, 500, etc.)
3. **Check response**:
   * Response body (JSON with success/data)
   * Response headers (Cache-Control, CORS headers)
   * Timing (should be \< 100ms with cache)

**Check timing**:

* **Cached response**: \< 50ms (backend cache hit)
* **Fresh query**: 100-500ms (Shopify API query)
* **Timeout**: > 5000ms (network or backend issue)

## Performance Issues

### Slow Pricing Resolution

**Symptoms**:

* Pricing takes several seconds to appear
* Widget shows loading state for extended time

**Possible Causes**:

1. **Slow network**: Check network speed
2. **Backend not caching**: Should see 15-minute cache headers
3. **Shopify API slow**: Check Shopify status
4. **Large customer base**: Many customers slows GraphQL query

**Diagnostic Steps**:

**Solutions**:

1. **Verify backend caching** is enabled (15-minute TTL)
2. **Check Shopify API status**: [status.shopify.com](https://www.shopify.com/admin/settings/general)
3. **Optimize customer query**: Ensure Shopify customer tags are indexed
4. **Contact Weevio support**: If persistent slow performance

### Widget Not Loading

**Symptoms**:

* Product page renders but no pricing widget appears
* No widget logs in console

**Diagnostic Steps**:

**Solutions**:

1. **Add widget container** to product template:

2. **Deploy widget script** (see [Deployment Guide](/docs/punchout/deployment))
3. **Fix JavaScript errors** blocking widget initialization
4. **Verify widget initialization code** is present and correct

## Error Messages Reference

### Console Error Messages

| Error Message | Meaning | Solution |
|--------------|---------|----------|
|

| Tracker script error | Check tracker script installation |
|

| Browser doesn't support sessionStorage | Use modern browser |
|

| Session > 12 hours old | Refresh session by clicking from SAP Ariba |
|

| API error or timeout | Check network, verify API endpoint |
|

| No matching customer tag | Tag customer correctly |
|

| Duplicate buyer IDs | Remove duplicate tags |
|

| Resolution failed, showing default | Check previous errors for root cause |

### API Error Responses

| Response | Meaning | Action |
|----------|---------|--------|
|

| Customer not tagged | Add/fix customer tag |
|

title: Weevio Store
description: Manage orders, track inventory, and streamline your retail operations with Weevio Cloud Store Management.
content:
Weevio Store is the central hub for managing your retail operations. Built to integrate seamlessly with PIMS Platinum and Gold systems, it provides powerful tools for order management, customer tracking, and real-time inventory synchronization.

## Overview

Weevio Store transforms your PIMS system into a modern, cloud-based retail management platform. Whether you're processing in-store sales, managing online orders, or tracking customer interactions, Weevio Store gives you the tools you need to run your business efficiently.

### Key Capabilities

* **Order Management**: Create, edit, and track customer orders with real-time inventory validation
* **PIMS Integration**: Automatic synchronization with your PIMS system ensures data consistency
* **Multi-Channel Support**: Handle orders from multiple sales channels in one unified interface
* **Customer Tracking**: Access complete customer history and information alongside orders
* **Real-Time Updates**: Changes sync instantly between Weevio Store and your PIMS system

## Core Features

### Additional Features

Weevio Store includes additional powerful features (documentation coming soon):

* **Service Repair Orders (SROs)**: Complete tracking and management of repair orders with status updates and customer communication
* **Customer Management**: Comprehensive customer profiles with complete transaction history and contact management
* **Inventory Management**: Real-time stock tracking, low inventory alerts, and product catalog management
* **Reporting & Analytics**: Sales reports, customer insights, and business performance analytics

## How It Works

### PIMS Integration

Weevio Store connects directly to your PIMS Platinum or Gold system through secure APIs. This integration provides:

1. **Real-Time Sync**: Product data, pricing, and inventory levels stay current
2. **Bidirectional Updates**: Changes made in Weevio Store or PIMS are reflected in both systems
3. **Data Consistency**: Single source of truth for customer and order information
4. **Automatic Backups**: All transactions are recorded in your PIMS database

### Workflow Integration

Weevio Store fits seamlessly into your existing operations:

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

Before using Weevio Store, ensure you have:

* Active Weevio Cloud account
* PIMS Platinum system
* API access credentials for your PIMS system
* Stable internet connection for real-time synchronization

### Quick Start

## What's Next?

Ready to start managing your store? Begin with the [Orders Management](/docs/store/orders) guide to learn how to process customer transactions, manage inventory, and provide excellent customer service.
